summary: Converted more fighter code from assembly to C for Donkey Kong, Samus, Mr. Game & Watch, and Crazy Hand while keeping the DOL matching. The PR removed matched leading routines from the corresponding asm files, added new C/header files and obj_files.mk entries, and expanded fighter.h special-attribute layouts needed to express those routines.
what_changed: Added C object entries for ftdonkey2.c, ftsamus.c, ftgamewatch.c, and ftcrazyhand.c in obj_files.mk before the remaining asm objects.

Removed matched assembly for Donkey routines func_8010D774, func_8010D7A8, func_8010D888, func_8010D8D0, func_8010D918, func_8010D96C, and ftDonkey_OnLoad from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdonkey_2.s and reimplemented them in src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey2.c.
Added ftDonkeyAttributes in src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey.h with offset-style fields x0 through x2C and filler to preserve size.

Removed ftGameWatch_OnDeath and ftGameWatch_OnLoad assembly and added C implementations in src/melee/ft/chara/ftgamewatch.c, including item reset/setup calls and Game & Watch special-attribute initialization.
Added ftGameWatchAttributes in ftgamewatch.h with offset-style fields and filler.

Removed ftCrazyhand_OnDeath and ftCrazyhand_OnLoad assembly and added C implementations in src/melee/ft/chara/ftcrazyhand.c, including PUSH_ATTRS, item registration IDs 0x7F through 0x81, flag setup, position initialization, and masterhand/crazyhand special-attribute assignments.
Added a large ftCrazyHandAttributes layout in ftcrazyhand.h using offset-based field names and Vec2/Vec3 members.

Added ftSamus_OnDeath and ftSamus_OnLoad in src/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.c and removed their assembly from ftsamus.s; OnLoad registers Samus item IDs 0x5D through 0x60 and sets fighter flag x2224_flag.bits.b7.
Renamed an ftsamus.s local label reference from lbl_80128428 to func_80128428 and left that function in asm, suggesting only part of Samus was converted in this PR.
Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h SpecialAttrs_DK, SpecialAttrs_Samus, SpecialAttrs_Gaw, and SpecialAttrs_Crazyhand from opaque filler into offset-labeled fields needed by the new C code.
Changed Fighter::x2CC from s32 to void* and split part of the filler around 0x5C8 to expose x5C8, used by Game & Watch item state.
Added a union over x610_color_rgba allowing access to x610 and x614 scalar fields, used by Game & Watch OnLoad.
Removed redundant includes from ftpikachu.h, leaving ftcommon.h and lbvector.h.
smart_moves: Used the existing PUSH_ATTRS macro for copied fighter attribute blocks instead of manually spelling the mtctr/lwzu/stwu copy loops seen in the removed assembly.
Kept asm fallback files present and only removed the ranges that had C equivalents, allowing incremental matching without requiring the entire fighter file to be decompiled at once.
Added C objects immediately before the corresponding asm objects in obj_files.mk, a standard decomp tactic for replacing leading symbols while preserving remaining assembly order.
Exposed only the fighter.h fields needed by the new C, using offset-style names when semantic names were not yet known. This enabled matching progress without overclaiming meanings.

Converted repeated item registration calls directly into indexed item-list accesses, preserving visible item ID constants such as Game & Watch 0x72-0x7A/0x7C, Samus 0x5D-0x60, and Crazy Hand 0x7F-0x81.
For Donkey Kong item/held-item behavior, modeled the assembly branch table as a switch over func_8026B320 results with cases 1 through 4.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter OnLoad routines, define the fighter-specific ext_attr struct even if most fields only have offset names; typed access is often enough to eliminate large attr-copy assembly blocks via PUSH_ATTRS.
Character OnDeath/OnLoad functions often require updates to both the character C file and the global Fighter special-attributes union in fighter.h.
Leaving unknown fields as xNN offset names is preferable to inventing semantics; this PR used names like x222C, x2238, and x5C8 where intent was not fully known.
Build-order placement matters when replacing asm with C: add the new C object before the old asm object so the matched leading functions resolve from C while later functions remain in asm.
Small header cleanups can be paired with matching work when a common header such as ftcommon.h already provides needed Dolphin/GObj types.
Label-to-function promotion in remaining asm, as seen with lbl_80128428 becoming func_80128428, can unblock relocations when C or clearer symbol references need a callable function symbol.
naming_conventions: Fighter lifecycle routines use character-prefixed names such as ftDonkey_OnLoad, ftSamus_OnDeath, ftGameWatch_OnLoad, and ftCrazyhand_OnLoad.
Character attribute structs follow typedef struct _ftCharacterAttributes { ... } ftCharacterAttributes, e.g. ftDonkeyAttributes, ftSamusAttributes, ftGameWatchAttributes, and ftCrazyHandAttributes.
Unknown struct fields are named by offset, such as x0, x2C, x222C, x2238, x5C8, and x2D4_specialAttributes.
Special-attribute structs in fighter.h use struct SpecialAttrs_Character naming, including SpecialAttrs_DK, SpecialAttrs_Samus, SpecialAttrs_Gaw, and SpecialAttrs_Crazyhand.
Vector fields include positional suffixes when only the type/role is apparent, e.g. x30_pos2, x40_pos, x88_pos, x110_pos, and x2240_pos.
Existing unknown function names remained address-derived, e.g. func_8010D7A8, func_8026B3F8, and func_80074A4C, rather than being renamed without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly attr-copy loops with PUSH_ATTRS(fighter, AttributeType), matching the original behavior of copying ext_attr into fighter->x2D4_specialAttributes.
Modeled PowerPC bitfield rlwimi stores as named flag bit assignments such as fighter->x2222_flag.bits.b0, fighter->x2223_flag.bits.b1, fighter->x2224_flag.bits.b0, and ft->x2229_b5_no_normal_motion.
Used exact unsigned constants in item registration calls, e.g. 0x128U/0x129U/0x12AU for Donkey attribute loads and item IDs 0x5DU through 0x81U.
Preserved assembly-sensitive local stack behavior in Donkey's func_8010D7A8 with an unused local array, indicating a matching-driven source shape.
Converted a branch cascade in Donkey item handling into a switch statement that matched case dispatch for item states 1, 2, 3, and 4.
Kept the remaining Samus assembly and updated references from lbl_80128428 to func_80128428 rather than forcing an incomplete conversion.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The only human body text says: "started more fighter files, DOL is matching".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8010D7A8 or other Donkey Kong item helper functions to semantic names.
Find later PRs that complete the remaining ftsamus.s functions around func_80128428.
Find PRs that refine ftCrazyHandAttributes or SpecialAttrs_Crazyhand field names beyond offset-based placeholders.
Find PRs that verify or fix Fighter offsets around x5C8 and the x610/x614 union introduced for Game & Watch.
Find matching examples that use PUSH_ATTRS for fighter OnLoad conversions.
